matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a BRCA1 homologous-recombination thesis: the individual may carry a signal relevant to DNA double-strand break repair, replication-fork protection, hereditary breast/ovarian cancer review, or HRD pathway biology. Only externally classified pathogenic or likely pathogenic BRCA1 variants should be escalated toward clinical hereditary-cancer risk interpretation.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed BRCA1 c.68_69delAG / 185delAG dosage suggests this sample carries the BRCA1 prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a BRCA1 homologous-recombination thesis: the individual may carry a signal relevant to DNA double-strand break repair, replication-fork protection, hereditary breast/ovarian cancer review, or HRD pathway biology. Only externally classified pathogenic or likely pathogenic BRCA1 variants should be escalated toward clinical hereditary-cancer risk interpretation. This specific marker is curated as BRCA1 frameshift founder marker. BRCA1 c.68_69delAG / 185delAG is curated as a BRCA1 marker for Hereditary breast and ovarian cancer context, Homologous recombination repair deficiency. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the homologous recombination repair, DNA double-strand break response, replication-fork protection, and genome stability pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched BRCA1 c.68_69delAG / 185delAG at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the BRCA1 prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag homologous recombination repair, DNA double-strand break response, replication-fork protection, and genome stability.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a BRCA1 homologous-recombination thesis: the individual may carry a signal relevant to DNA double-strand break repair, replication-fork protection, hereditary breast/ovarian cancer review, or HRD pathway biology. Only externally classified pathogenic or likely pathogenic BRCA1 variants should be escalated toward clinical hereditary-cancer risk interpretation. An observed BRCA1 variant places this sample in the curated BRCA1 research context. The local BRCA1 bundle is DNA-repair and hereditary cancer oriented. Pathogenic BRCA1 alleles are clinically important, but app-level matches remain research/triage context until confirmed by clinical-grade variant interpretation. BRCA1 truncating, splice, and damaging missense variants can compromise homologous recombination and hereditary breast/ovarian cancer biology.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: Hereditary breast and ovarian cancer context

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a BRCA1 variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a BRCA1 homologous-recombination thesis: the individual may carry a signal relevant to DNA double-strand break repair, replication-fork protection, hereditary breast/ovarian cancer review, or HRD pathway biology. Only externally classified pathogenic or likely pathogenic BRCA1 variants should be escalated toward clinical hereditary-cancer risk interpretation. BRCA1 methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a homologous recombination repair, DNA double-strand break response, replication-fork protection, and genome stability gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data. The local BRCA1 bundle is DNA-repair and hereditary cancer oriented. Pathogenic BRCA1 alleles are clinically important, but app-level matches remain research/triage context until confirmed by clinical-grade variant interpretation. BRCA1 truncating, splice, and damaging missense variants can compromise homologous recombination and hereditary breast/ovarian cancer biology.
